data.title: Tokeny Proposes Regulatory Suggestions for Digital Securities to SEC
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Tokeny, a provider of financial institution blockchain operating systems, has recently sent a letter to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's crypto working group, offering recommendations on digital securities regulation. The company outlined four main suggestions in its correspondence: 

Firstly, Tokeny advocates for the application of consistent rules to identical assets, regardless of their technological form, suggesting that regulatory approaches should not change based on the technology used. Secondly, the company calls for the recognition of tokens as legitimate proof of ownership, emphasizing their legal status. 

Thirdly, Tokeny advises against imposing additional custodial requirements on digital securities, aiming to streamline regulatory processes. Lastly, the company recommends allowing the use of blockchain as a settlement layer, ensuring that it does not hinder transaction discovery. 

These proposals are part of Tokeny's efforts to influence the regulatory landscape for digital securities, seeking to align technological advancements with existing legal frameworks.

data.title: After the release of PPI data, the probability of the Fed cutting interest rates in March is only 1%.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The U.S. PPI in February recorded the smallest increase since July 2024. According to CME's "Fed Watch" data, after the release of the PPI data, the probability of the Federal Reserve keeping interest rates unchanged in March is 99.0%, and the probability of a 25 basis point rate cut is only 1.0%. There are about 6 days left until the next FOMC meeting.

data.title: Bybit Card has issued 1.5 million cards in its second anniversary since its launch, and has launched a number of product innovations and market expansion plans in collaboration with Mastercard
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Bybit Card recently held a two-year anniversary event at Bybit's Dubai headquarters, announcing that it has issued more than 1.5 million cards worldwide. At the event, Bybit and Mastercard announced a number of product innovations and market expansion plans, including: 
-Customized limited edition metal cards: expected to be launched in the first half of 2025. 
-Improved user experience: All card holders will enjoy an improved rewards program. 
-Strengthened anti-fraud protection: further ensure the security of user funds. 
-Redesigned intuitive dashboard. 
-Wearable device connection: convenient for users to manage assets. 
-Free family card: meet the diverse needs of users and their families.

data.title: U.S. Senate Banking Committee to Review Stablecoin Regulation Proposal
data.description: According to BlockBeats, FOX Business journalist Eleanor Terrett announced on social media that the U.S. Senate Banking Committee is set to conduct its initial review of a stablecoin regulatory framework proposed by Republican Senator Bill Hagerty. The legislation aims to establish guidelines for the oversight and management of stablecoins within the financial sector.

data.title: Earlier this month, Texas prosecutors ordered the seizure of approximately 749 Bitcoins associated with Silk Road.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Federal prosecutors in the Western District of Texas ordered the confiscation of approximately 749 bitcoins related to the Silk Road, worth approximately US$62.5 million, earlier this month. It is reported that these assets are related to illegal activities carried out by two men, one of whom is a drug dealer on the Silk Road and the other assists him in laundering cryptocurrencies. The two of them transferred the bitcoins obtained through Silk Road transactions through multiple different accounts and exchanged them for cash through LocalBitcoins. Part of the funds eventually flowed into the crypto exchange Gemini, which reported the deposits to law enforcement. (Forbes)

data.title: CZ: The Wall Street Journal report distorted the facts and did not discuss the Binance US transaction
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Binance co-founder CZ posted on the X platform that the Wall Street Journal report distorted the facts, they worked hard to make up a story to report, and they have not discussed the Binance US transaction with anyone yet.

data.title: Rootstock Reveals Three Major Upgrades, 60% Lower Transaction Fees
data.description:
Rootstock, a Bitcoin sidechain for smart contracts, announced a major three-pronged upgrade that boosts its scalability, security, and decentralization.
The post Rootstock Reveals Three Major Upgrades, 60% Lower Transaction Fees appeared first on Cryptonews.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/rootstock-reveals-three-major-upgrades-60-lower-transaction-fees/

data.title: The U.S. Senate Banking Committee will discuss and review the stablecoin regulatory framework and the FIRM Act today
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Eleanor Terrett, a crypto journalist, on the X platform, the U.S. Senate Banking Committee will discuss and review Republican Senator Bill Hagerty’s stablecoin regulatory framework bill today. In addition, it will also discuss the FIRM Act, which will prevent regulators from using “reputational risks” to implement de-banking practices.

data.title: Circle plans to bring Hashnote tokenized money market fund under Bermuda regulation
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: USDC stablecoin issuer Circle plans to bring its recently acquired Hashnote tokenized money market fund under the regulatory scope of Bermuda. It is reported that Hashnote has issued the largest tokenized money market fund USYC, with an asset size of nearly US$1 billion. Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire believes that operating in Bermuda is a "natural choice" for USYC given Bermuda's role in digital asset regulation. (Coindesk)
